stories.title: Djokovic Stunned by Prizmic — First Rome Opening-Round Loss in 18 Appearances

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: SHOCKING. Dino Prizmic (20, qualifier) defeats Novak Djokovic 2-6, 6-2, 6-4 in Rome. First time in 18 appearances Djokovic has lost in the opening round. A visibly hampered 6-time champion exits early. #tennis #ItalianOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Djokovic after Rome exit: "I'm pleased at least that I fought 'til the end." First clay match in 57 days. Right shoulder not right. Roland Garros in 15 days. The question now: is Paris still realistic? #Djokovic #RolandGarros

article.headline: Djokovic Falls to Prizmic in Rome — First Opening-Round Loss in 18 Appearances

research.facts:
Djokovic Stunned by Prizmic in Rome
**Source 1:** ATP Tour official — "Dino Prizmic stuns Novak Djokovic in Rome" (atptour.com/en/news/djokovic-prizmic-rome-2026-friday) — HIGH confidence
**Source 2:** Olympics.com — "Italian Open 2026: Dino Prižmić secures biggest win of his career against Novak Djokovic in three-set thriller" — HIGH confidence
**Verified facts:**
- Score: 2-6, 6-2, 6-4 — HIGH (2 sources)
- Duration: 2 hours 15-17 minutes — MEDIUM (slight variance between sources, ATP says 2:17, Olympics says 2:15)
- Prizmic is 20 years old, Croatian, came through qualifying — HIGH
- Djokovic's first-ever opening round loss in Rome (was 18-0 in Rome openers) — HIGH (ATP official)
- Djokovic had not played in 57 days (right shoulder injury, last played Indian Wells) — HIGH
- Djokovic physically hampered: hands on knees between points, slow to chair at changeovers — HIGH
- Djokovic quote: "I'm pleased at least that I fought 'til the end" — HIGH (ATP)
- Djokovic pre-match: "I'm not completely happy with my physical condition" — HIGH
- Djokovic is 6-time Rome champion — HIGH (from prior pipeline history)
- Prizmic advanced through qualifying — HIGH (multiple sources)

stories.title: Sinner's Home Debut Tonight — Foro Italico and the Golden Masters Hunt

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Jannik Sinner steps onto Foro Italico TONIGHT vs Sebastian Ofner. 30-2 in 2026. Five straight Masters 1000 titles. Eight of nine won. The only M1000 missing: Rome. Home crowd. Golden Masters. Let's go. #Sinner #ItalianOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: No Italian man has won Rome since Adriano Panatta in 1976. Tonight Jannik Sinner faces Sebastian Ofner at Foro Italico — home crowd roaring, Career Golden Masters on the line. The stage is set. #Sinner #tennis

article.headline: Sinner Steps onto Foro Italico Stage — The Golden Masters Mission Begins

research.facts:
Sinner vs Ofner Rome R2
**Source 1:** ATP Tour — "When will Jannik Sinner play his first match in Rome?" — HIGH
**Source 2:** Puntodebreak — "Schedule and where to watch Jannik Sinner's debut at ATP Rome 2026 against Sebastian Ofner" — HIGH
**Verified facts:**
- Sinner match scheduled not before 19:00 local time on May 9 — HIGH
- Sinner record in 2026: 30-2 — HIGH (multiple prior pipeline sources, confirmed)
- Sinner has won 5 consecutive Masters 1000 titles — HIGH (prior pipeline coverage)
- Sinner has 8 of 9 Masters 1000 titles (Career Golden Masters quest) — HIGH (ATP Tour official)
- Rome is the only M1000 Sinner hasn't won — HIGH
- Ofner defeated Michelsen in R1 to set up the match — HIGH (Puntodebreak)
- Sinner H2H vs Ofner: no prior matches found — MEDIUM (not independently verified)
- Sinner 28-match Masters 1000 win streak entering Rome — MEDIUM (was 28 per May 8 pipeline, needs confirmation this is current)

stories.title: WTA Rome R3 — Sabalenka, Gauff, Paolini, Andreeva All in Action Saturday

x_posts.text: WTA Rome R3 — Saturday is stacked: Sabalenka vs Cirstea. Gauff vs Sierra. Paolini vs Mertens (defending champ). Andreeva vs Golubic. Projected QF: Sabalenka vs Gauff. Clay season's best day yet. #WTA #ItalianOpen

research.facts:
WTA Rome R3 Day
**Source 1:** WTA Official (wtatennis.com) — R3 schedule — HIGH
**Source 2:** Tennis Tonic — "Sabalenka, Gauff, Jovic and Townsend to play on Saturday" — HIGH
**Verified facts:**
- Sabalenka (No. 1) vs Cirstea (No. 26) — R3 scheduled May 9 at 3:30 PM — HIGH
- Gauff (No. 3) vs Solana Sierra (ARG) — R3 scheduled May 9 — HIGH
- Paolini (No. 9) vs Mertens (No. 21) — R3 scheduled May 9 — HIGH
- Andreeva (No. 7 or 8) vs Golubic — R3 scheduled May 9 — HIGH
- Sabalenka def. Krejcikova 6-2, 6-3 in R2 — HIGH (from May 8 pipeline/WTA)
- Gauff def. Valentova 6-3, 6-4 in R2 — HIGH (Tennis Tonic)
- Paolini def. Jeanjean 6-7(4), 6-2, 6-4 in R2 — HIGH (from May 8 pipeline)
- Sabalenka and Gauff projected to meet in QF — MEDIUM (draw projection, not confirmed)
- Paolini is defending champion — HIGH (pipeline history)

stories.title: ATP Rome R2 Day — Medvedev, Zverev and the Race to Stop Sinner

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: ATP Rome R2 is live. Medvedev vs Machac on Campo Centrale. Zverev looking to back up Friday's 7-5, 6-3 win over Altmaier. Djokovic is out. The draw is Sinner's to lose. #ATPRome #tennis

stories.title: Roland Garros in 15 Days — Djokovic Exit Reshapes the Paris Picture

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: 15 days to Roland Garros. Alcaraz: OUT (wrist). Djokovic: doubtful after Rome exit. Sinner: 30-2, five consecutive M1000 titles, chasing Career Grand Slam. The path to Paris has never looked clearer for No. 1. #RolandGarros

article.headline: Roland Garros 2026: With Alcaraz Out and Djokovic Doubtful, Sinner's Path to Paris Has Never Been Clearer

Roland Garros Preview
**Source 1:** Roland Garros official site — HIGH
**Source 2:** LTA UK preview — HIGH

- Roland Garros qualifying starts May 18 — HIGH
- Roland Garros main draw starts May 24 (Sunday) — HIGH
- Tournament ends June 7 — HIGH
- Alcaraz out with wrist injury — HIGH (confirmed multiple prior pipelines)
- Swiatek is 4-time Roland Garros champion — HIGH (prior pipeline)
- Sinner has never won Roland Garros (Career Grand Slam pursuit) — HIGH
- Djokovic has 3 Roland Garros titles (2016, 2021, 2023) — HIGH
- Gauff won Roland Garros 2023 — MEDIUM (verify — she won it in 2023, confirmed)
- Sabalenka has not won Roland Garros — HIGH
